summary: Fixed the generated progress site's font asset selection by switching from the medium A-OTF Folk Pro font to the heavy font in both the GitHub pages setup script and Svelte/CSS font-face declaration. The PR also removed a stale duplicate @font-face rule, corrected the CSS font format to opentype, initialized Highcharts accessibility, sorted chart point arrays by x value before rendering, and reformatted the progress-site package-lock.json.
what_changed: In .github/packages/gen-pages/setup.sh, the font asset copied into /opt/assets/progress changed from '/tmp/A-OTF Folk Pro/A-OTF Folk Pro M/A-OTF Folk Pro M.otf' to '/tmp/A-OTF Folk Pro/A-OTF Folk Pro H/A-OTF Folk Pro H.otf'.
In tools/progress-site/src/global.css, a duplicate top-level @font-face rule referencing './A-OTF-Folk Pro.otf' was removed.

The remaining @font-face declaration for 'A-OTF Folk Pro' changed from font-weight 400 and '$lib/extern/A-OTF Folk Pro M.otf' format('otf') to font-weight 700 and '$lib/extern/A-OTF Folk Pro H.otf' format('opentype').
In ProgressPanel.svelte, Highcharts accessibility module import and initialization were added.
ProgressPanel.svelte introduced a singular Point type alias, reused it for Points, and added comparePoint to sort codePct and dataPct by x before chart creation.

tools/progress-site/package-lock.json had 3473 added and 3473 deleted lines; the excerpt indicates broad lockfile formatting churn, primarily tabs changed to spaces, with no clear dependency-version change visible in the provided evidence.
smart_moves: The font fix updated both sides of the asset pipeline: the page-generation setup script now installs the H font file, and the app CSS now requests that same H font file.
Removing the obsolete @font-face rule avoids ambiguity between multiple declarations for the same 'A-OTF Folk Pro' family.
Using format('opentype') instead of format('otf') is a more standard CSS descriptor for an .otf font asset.
Sorting chart point arrays by x before handing them to Highcharts reduces dependence on input JSON iteration order and should make the progress chart render more predictably.
decomp_lessons: Even non-code project infrastructure, such as the progress site, needs asset paths kept in sync between generation scripts and frontend CSS; updating only one side can silently break generated pages.
When changing generated or build-pipeline assets, prefer exact upstream asset names in both scripts and CSS so future maintainers can trace the file source.
Large lockfile diffs may be pure formatting churn; future PRs should call this out explicitly or avoid it when unrelated to the functional fix.
naming_conventions: Font asset names preserve the upstream family and weight naming: 'A-OTF Folk Pro H.otf' for the heavy weight and previously 'A-OTF Folk Pro M.otf' for medium.
The Svelte component uses a singular type alias 'Point' and plural collection alias 'Points', making the comparePoint helper signature clearer.
The comparator helper is named by behavior and input type: comparePoint(a: Point, b: Point).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or decomp matching tactics were involved; this PR was a progress-site tooling/UI fix.
Tooling tactic: update the GitHub Pages setup script's downloaded font path and the frontend @font-face declaration together so generated assets and runtime references match.
follow_up_queries: Check whether tools/progress-site/package-lock.json changed only indentation or also dependency metadata.
Search later PRs for additional progress-site font or generated pages asset fixes.
Verify whether Highcharts accessibility initialization was added to satisfy a warning, test, or site audit, since no PR comments/body explain it in this slice.
